chapter_summary: Coming down from the mountain, Jesus heals a leper with a touch, heals the centurion's servant (and marvels at the Gentile's faith), heals Peter's mother-in-law and a great crowd at evening, responds to two would-be followers about the cost of discipleship, stills the storm on the Sea of Galilee, and heals two demon-possessed men in the country of the Gadarenes — casting the demons into a herd of pigs.
main_lesson: Jesus has power over every kind of sickness, every kind of fear, and every kind of dark power — and He uses that power with great love and gentleness.

memory_verse.kjv: But the men marvelled, saying, What manner of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him!
memory_verse.little_bible: The disciples were amazed and asked each other, 'Who IS this? Even the wind and the waves do what He says!' Jesus has power over everything.

parent_guide: This chapter is a showcase of Jesus' authority — over disease (leper, paralysis, fever), creation (the storm), and spiritual forces (the Gadarene demoniacs). The centurion episode is particularly rich: a Gentile military officer shows greater faith than anyone in Israel, recognising authority structures because he himself operates under them. This is a perfect opportunity to talk about faith and trust. The storm stilling can be linked to Psalm 107:28-30 for older children. Handle the Gadarene demons age-appropriately: Jesus has power over anything dark or scary.
application_for_children: Nothing is too hard for Jesus. When we feel scared or sick or things feel out of control, we can ask Jesus for help — He has power over everything.

verses.kjv: When he was come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him.
verses.little_bible: After Jesus finished teaching on the mountain, huge crowds of people followed Him down. Everyone wanted to be near Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Big crowds followed Jesus down the mountain.
verses.meaning: The crowds had just heard the Sermon on the Mount and were drawn to Jesus. His teaching had authority unlike anything they had heard before.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to follow You just like those crowds did. Help me stay close to You every day.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think so many people wanted to follow Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like for a family to 'follow Jesus' in everyday life, not just on Sundays?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today something you love about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking down a hillside path with a large, joyful crowd of people of all ages following closely behind Him.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there came a leper and worshipped him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
verses.little_bible: A man with a terrible skin disease came to Jesus, kneeled down, and said, 'Lord, I know You can make me well — if You want to.' He trusted Jesus completely.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A sick man knelt and said, 'You can heal me if you want to!'
verses.meaning: Lepers were completely cut off from society. This man's approach to Jesus showed both humility ('if thou wilt') and great faith ('thou canst'). He did not demand — he trusted.
verses.memory_phrase: Lord, if You will, You can
verses.prayer: Lord, I trust that You are able to help me. Help me come to You with that same faith.
verses.discussion_question: What is brave about what this man did? Why might it have been scary to approach Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: The man said 'if You want to.' He trusted Jesus' choice as well as Jesus' power. How do we pray when we are not sure what God will do?
verses.do_it_today: Pray and tell God about something you need help with, and then say 'I trust You, Lord' at the end.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man in worn clothing kneeling before Jesus with hands outstretched, visible marks of leprosy on his skin, his eyes full of hope.

verses.kjv: And Jesus put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will; be thou clean. And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
verses.little_bible: Jesus reached out His hand and touched the man — nobody else would touch a leper! Jesus said, 'Yes, I want to. Be clean!' And right away the man was completely healed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus touched him and said 'Be clean!' — and he was healed instantly!
verses.meaning: Jesus did not have to touch the leper to heal him. He chose to, showing that He was not repelled by this man's condition. The touch communicated love and dignity before the healing even happened.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for not being afraid to come close to people who are hurting. Help me be kind like You.
verses.discussion_question: Everyone else avoided lepers, but Jesus touched him. What does that tell you about how Jesus sees people?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus could have healed from far away with just a word. Why might He have chosen to touch this man first?
verses.do_it_today: Find someone who feels left out today and do something kind to include them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gently placing His hand on the shoulder of a kneeling man, a warm glow spreading from the touch as the man's skin becomes clear and healthy.

verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, See thou tell no man; but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told him, 'Don't go around telling everyone just yet. First, go show yourself to the priest and bring the offering God's law requires — that will be a witness to them.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Go to the priest first. Follow God's rules.'
verses.meaning: Jesus respected the Jewish law. The healed man needed to be officially declared clean by a priest so he could return to normal life in the community. Jesus cared about his full restoration.
verses.prayer: God, help me do things the right way, not just the fast way. Thank You for caring about every part of my life.
verses.discussion_question: Why was it important for the healed man to go to the priest?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus healed the man — but then pointed him toward the steps to restore his place in society. How does God's help often work alongside practical next steps?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something good you started but didn't finish? Take one step to finish it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: The healed man walking happily on a road toward a temple building, clean clothing, carrying a small offering, beaming with joy.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus was entered into Capernaum, there came unto him a centurion, beseeching him,
verses.little_bible: When Jesus arrived in Capernaum, a Roman army captain came to Him and urgently asked for help. This soldier commanded one hundred men — but he came humbly to Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A Roman army captain came to Jesus, asking for help.
verses.meaning: A centurion was a respected Roman military officer. For him to come personally and humbly to a Jewish teacher was remarkable — showing he had already recognised something extraordinary about Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, no matter who I am or what I do, help me come to You humbly when I need help.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it surprising that a powerful Roman soldier would come to ask Jesus for help?
verses.family_discussion: The centurion had earthly power but still recognised he needed Jesus. What does that teach us about true power?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you have been trying to handle alone? Ask God for help with it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A Roman soldier in armour, helmet under his arm, approaching Jesus on a town street with a respectful, urgent expression.

verses.kjv: And saying, Lord, my servant lieth at home sick of the palsy, grievously tormented.
verses.little_bible: The soldier said, 'Lord, my servant is at home, completely paralysed — he is suffering terribly.' He cared deeply about his servant, even though servants were often treated as unimportant.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He said, 'Lord, my servant is very sick at home.'
verses.meaning: Paralysis ('palsy') was incurable at the time. The centurion did not come for himself but for someone in his household he clearly cared about — showing an unusual compassion for the era.
verses.prayer: Lord, teach me to care about other people's pain, not just my own. Help me pray for others.
verses.discussion_question: What does it tell you about the centurion that he cared so much for his servant?
verses.family_discussion: People of power often ignored servants' pain. How can we follow this centurion's example and notice who around us might be hurting?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person you know who is sick or struggling today.
verses.illustration_prompt: The centurion gesturing toward a distant house, worry on his face, speaking earnestly to Jesus.

verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, I will come and heal him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered right away, 'Yes, I will come and heal him.' Jesus never hesitates to help when someone comes to Him with faith.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I will come and heal him!'
verses.meaning: Jesus' response is immediate and willing — no questions, no conditions. This reflects His nature: ready and willing to help those who come to Him in faith.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, that You are always ready to help me. Your answer to our need is always 'Yes, I will come.'
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said 'I will' right away. Does that surprise you? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was willing to go to a Gentile soldier's home — crossing cultural boundaries. What does this tell us about who Jesus came for?
verses.do_it_today: When a friend asks you for help today, say yes as quickly as you can.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus stepping forward with a warm, decisive expression, the centurion looking relieved and grateful.

verses.kjv: The centurion answered and said, Lord, I am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof: but speak the word only, and my servant shall be healed.
verses.little_bible: The soldier said, 'Lord, I am not good enough for You to come to my house. But just say the word from here, and I know my servant will be healed.' This was amazing faith!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He said, 'Just say the word, Lord — that's enough to heal him!'
verses.meaning: The centurion understood that Jesus' authority did not depend on proximity. A word from Jesus was enough. This is one of the most remarkable expressions of faith in the Gospels.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me trust that Your word is enough. I don't need to see everything to believe You.
verses.discussion_question: The centurion believed Jesus could heal without even being there. How strong is your faith?
verses.family_discussion: This verse is echoed in church liturgy: 'Lord, I am not worthy.' What does true humility before God look like, even for people who seem very capable?
verses.do_it_today: Practice saying this: 'Lord, just speak — Your word is enough for me.'
verses.illustration_prompt: The centurion with one hand raised, humbly declining Jesus' advance, his face full of reverent trust.

verses.kjv: For I am a man under authority, having soldiers under me: and I say to this man, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.
verses.little_bible: The soldier explained, 'I understand authority. When I give orders, soldiers obey. So I know that when You speak, everything obeys You — even sickness!' He understood who Jesus really was.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He understood that when Jesus speaks, everything must obey.
verses.meaning: The centurion reasoned from his own experience with authority: if his commands worked over soldiers, Jesus' commands — backed by God's authority — would work over any situation. This is brilliant, humble reasoning.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have all authority. Help me trust and obey You like a soldier trusts their commander.
verses.discussion_question: How did the centurion's job help him understand who Jesus was?
verses.family_discussion: The centurion connected his everyday experience with spiritual truth. How can things in your daily life help you understand God better?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one way you can 'obey' Jesus today — do one thing He asks in the Bible.
verses.illustration_prompt: The centurion speaking with confidence, soldiers in the background at attention, illustrating the chain of command he describes.

verses.kjv: When Jesus heard it, he marvelled, and said to them that followed, Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus heard the soldier's words, He was amazed! Jesus told the crowd, 'I tell you the truth — I have not found anyone in all of Israel with faith as great as this man's!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was amazed and said, 'I have never seen such great faith!'
verses.meaning: This is one of only two times in the Gospels where Jesus 'marvelled'. Remarkably, it was a Gentile, not an Israelite, who drew out this response. Faith, not ethnicity, is what moves Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, I want to have faith that amazes You! Grow my trust in You every day.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus was surprised. What does it mean that a Gentile soldier had more faith than the religious people of Israel?
verses.family_discussion: Faith can come from unexpected places. Have you ever seen great faith in someone you didn't expect?
verses.do_it_today: Write or draw what 'big faith' looks like to you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turning to the crowd with an expression of wonder and delight, gesturing toward the centurion.

verses.kjv: And I say unto you, That many shall come from the east and west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'People will come from all over the world — from every direction — and they will sit at the feast in God's kingdom with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People from everywhere will feast together in God's kingdom.
verses.meaning: Jesus uses the centurion's faith as a launching point to declare that the Kingdom of God is for all nations. The great patriarchs' table will include Gentile believers.
verses.memory_phrase: From east and west to God's feast
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Your family includes everyone! Help me welcome all kinds of people.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus says people from all over the world will be in heaven. How does that make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: God's kingdom is bigger than any one nation or people group. How does your family show that all people are welcome?
verses.do_it_today: Learn one fact about a country very different from yours and thank God for those people.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glorious banquet table with people of many different backgrounds and skin tones seated joyfully together, with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob at the head.

verses.kjv: But the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.little_bible: But Jesus warned that those who should have known better — and rejected Him — would be left outside in darkness, full of sadness and regret.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Those who reject Jesus will miss out on God's wonderful feast.
verses.meaning: This is a solemn warning. Religious privilege does not guarantee a place in the Kingdom. Those who assume their position is secure while rejecting faith and Jesus Himself will be shut out.
verses.memory_phrase: Privilege is not the same as faith
verses.prayer: Lord, I don't want to take my place in Your family for granted. Keep my heart truly trusting You.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between knowing about God and truly trusting Him?
verses.family_discussion: Growing up in a Christian home is a blessing, but it doesn't automatically make us right with God. How do we keep our faith real and personal?
verses.do_it_today: Talk honestly with God today — not just going through the motions, but really speaking to Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright, warm feast hall seen through a window, while a shadowy figure stands outside in the cold, looking in.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto the centurion, Go thy way; and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee. And his servant was healed in the selfsame hour.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told the soldier, 'Go home. Because you believed, it has been done.' And at that very moment — miles away — the servant was completely healed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said 'Go!' — and the servant was healed that very hour.
verses.meaning: Jesus honors faith with action. The healing was instantaneous and remote — matching exactly the centurion's own understanding of how authority works.
verses.memory_phrase: As you believed, so it is done
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You can do what seems impossible. Thank You that You reward real faith.
verses.discussion_question: The servant was healed miles away the moment Jesus spoke. What does that tell you about Jesus' power?
verses.family_discussion: The centurion believed before he saw any evidence. Can you think of a time when you trusted God before seeing the answer?
verses.do_it_today: Practice praying specifically and expectantly for something today — and write it down.
verses.illustration_prompt: The centurion receiving the news with joyful tears as a messenger runs toward him, while at the same moment a servant rises healthy from his bed in a distant house.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus was come into Peter's house, he saw his wife's mother laid, and sick of a fever.
verses.little_bible: Jesus went to Peter's home and found Peter's mother-in-law lying in bed with a bad fever. Jesus noticed her need right away.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: At Peter's house, Jesus saw his mother-in-law was sick with a fever.
verses.meaning: This is a small, domestic healing — not in public, not a dramatic scene. It shows that Jesus cares just as much about ordinary family situations as about public miracles.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You notice when people in my family are hurting. Thank You for caring about our home.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus helped someone in Peter's own house. What does that tell you about how personal Jesus' care is?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes the needs closest to us are the ones we overlook. How can we be more attentive to the people in our own home?
verses.do_it_today: Check in with each person in your home today: 'How are you really doing?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus entering a simple home, seeing a woman lying on a mat with a flushed face, family members nearby looking worried.

verses.kjv: And he touched her hand, and the fever left her: and she arose, and ministered unto them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus took her hand, and the fever left immediately. She got up — completely well — and began serving everyone. Healing led straight to helping others!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus touched her hand. The fever left and she got up to help everyone.
verses.meaning: The healing is instant and complete. Notably, she immediately begins to serve — this is the natural response to being healed by Jesus. Receiving His grace moves us toward serving others.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when You help me, I want to use that help to serve others too. Make me a giver, not just a receiver.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the first thing she did after being healed was to serve Jesus and the others?
verses.family_discussion: Being helped by Jesus naturally leads to serving. Where in your family life can you use God's blessing to bless others?
verses.do_it_today: Do one helpful thing for your family today without being asked.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman, bright-eyed and well, standing up from her mat, reaching out to offer food or drink to Jesus and the disciples with a joyful expression.

verses.kjv: When the even was come, they brought unto him many that were possessed with devils: and he cast out the spirits with his word, and healed all that were sick:
verses.little_bible: That evening, people brought many sick and troubled people to Jesus. He healed every single one. He spoke with authority and every dark spirit had to leave.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: That evening, Jesus healed everyone who was sick or troubled.
verses.meaning: The Sabbath ended at sunset, so people could travel and carry the sick. The scene became a mass healing — Jesus healed 'all', not just some. His power and willingness knew no limits.
verses.prayer: Lord, no one is too broken for You to heal. Thank You that You turn away no one who comes to You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus healed everyone that evening — not just some. What does 'all' tell you about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: People were brought to Jesus. Who in your community might need someone to bring them closer to Jesus right now?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone who doesn't know Jesus and pray for them by name today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A large crowd in the fading evening light, people being helped toward Jesus who stands at the centre, a golden glow around Him as He heals person after person.

verses.kjv: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying, Himself took our infirmities, and bare our sicknesses.
verses.little_bible: This fulfilled what the prophet Isaiah had written long ago: 'He Himself carried our weaknesses and bore our sicknesses.' Jesus was the One Isaiah had been talking about all along!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: This was what Isaiah the prophet said would happen about Jesus!
verses.meaning: Matthew quotes Isaiah 53:4 — a Suffering Servant passage. Jesus' healings were not incidental acts of kindness but part of a plan written centuries before. He carries our pain as His own.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You didn't just watch our pain from far away — You came and carried it. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: God wrote about what Jesus would do hundreds of years before Jesus was born. How does that make you feel about God's plan?
verses.family_discussion: Isaiah 53:4 connects Jesus' healings to the cross — He bears our sickness and our sin. How does knowing Jesus 'carries' your pain change how you feel when you're hurting?
verses.do_it_today: Read Isaiah 53:3-5 together as a family.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open ancient scroll with Isaiah's words, overlaid with a gentle image of Jesus with hands outstretched over a crowd of hurting people.

verses.kjv: Now when Jesus saw great multitudes about him, he gave commandment to depart unto the other side.
verses.little_bible: Jesus saw the huge crowds around Him and decided it was time to cross to the other side of the lake. He knew when to go and where to go next.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw the big crowds and said, 'Let's cross to the other side.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was not driven by crowd pressure. He moved according to purpose, not popularity. This shows His intentionality — He always knew where He needed to be.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me follow Your lead even when the crowd pulls another way. Give me wisdom to go where You go.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus chose to leave the crowd instead of staying?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus didn't let popularity decide His schedule. How do we decide what to do — by what feels popular or by what God is leading us toward?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God today: 'Where do You want me to go and what do You want me to do?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking purposefully toward a waiting boat on a shoreline, disciples following, a large crowd watching from behind.

verses.kjv: And a certain scribe came, and said unto him, Master, I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.
verses.little_bible: A teacher of the law came to Jesus and said enthusiastically, 'Teacher, I will follow You wherever You go!' He sounded very sure of himself.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A man said, 'I will follow You everywhere, Jesus!'
verses.meaning: The scribe's declaration sounds impressive but may have been impulsive. Jesus' response probes whether this man truly understood what following meant — it meant hardship, not comfort.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to follow You for real — not just when it's easy. Help me count the cost and still choose You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever said you would do something big and then found out it was harder than you thought?
verses.family_discussion: Enthusiasm is good, but following Jesus requires real commitment. How does your family prepare for the hard parts of following Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one hard thing about following Jesus and one reason why it's worth it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A confident-looking man in scholarly robes gesturing broadly toward Jesus with a declaration on his face, Jesus watching him thoughtfully.

verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.
verses.little_bible: Jesus replied, 'Foxes have dens and birds have nests — but I, the Son of Man, have no home to sleep in.' Jesus was telling him: following Me is not an easy or comfortable life.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Even animals have homes, but I have nowhere to sleep.'
verses.meaning: Jesus describes His own homelessness honestly. This is not a discouragement but a warning: discipleship means sharing in Jesus' vulnerability, not gaining worldly comfort.
verses.memory_phrase: Son of Man had no home
verses.prayer: Jesus, You left all the comforts of heaven for us. Help me be willing to give up comfort to follow You.
verses.discussion_question: What would it feel like to have no place to sleep every night? What did that cost Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was homeless, yet kings and generals came to Him. What does true greatness look like in God's kingdom?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God for your home today. Then pray for someone you know who doesn't have a safe place to sleep.
verses.illustration_prompt: A fox peering from its den and a bird settled in its nest, contrasted with Jesus sitting under the stars with no shelter, peaceful but vulnerable.

verses.kjv: And another of his disciples said unto him, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.
verses.little_bible: Another follower said, 'Lord, let me first go home and take care of burying my father.' He wanted to follow Jesus — but after he had handled something important first.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Another man said, 'Let me first go bury my father, then I'll follow.'
verses.meaning: Burying a parent was the highest family obligation in Jewish culture. This is not a wicked request — it is a deeply human one. But Jesus' answer shows that the Kingdom cannot always wait for human timelines.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not keep putting You second. I want to make following You my first priority.
verses.discussion_question: Why do we sometimes put 'I'll follow Jesus after…' in front of actually following Him?
verses.family_discussion: The man had a real and legitimate concern. How do we balance family duties with following Jesus? When does following Jesus have to come first?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you have been waiting to do for God? Take one step toward it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man looking torn, one hand reaching toward Jesus and the other pointing back toward a home in the distance.

verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto him, Follow me; and let the dead bury their dead.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to him, 'Follow Me. Let those who are spiritually dead take care of the dead. You — come and live.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Follow Me now. The living must follow the living.'
verses.meaning: Jesus' statement is deliberately jarring. 'The dead' likely refers to those who are spiritually dead — those without Kingdom life — to handle the affairs of physical death. The urgency of the Kingdom call is absolute.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me respond when You call — without delay and without excuses.
verses.discussion_question: Does Jesus' answer seem harsh? What do you think He was really trying to say?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes following Jesus requires a difficult choice between something good and something urgent. How does your family discern those moments?
verses.do_it_today: When you feel God nudging you to do something today, do it without putting it off.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus extending His hand toward the hesitant man with a compelling, urgent look, the road of discipleship stretching ahead.

verses.kjv: And when he was entered into a ship, his disciples followed him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus got into the boat and His disciples followed Him. They were learning: wherever Jesus goes, we go too.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus got in the boat and His disciples followed.
verses.meaning: Simple as this verse is, it is significant: the disciples followed into a situation they could not predict. Following Jesus means not knowing everything that lies ahead.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I will follow You even when I don't know what's coming. I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: The disciples didn't know a storm was coming when they got in the boat. How do you follow Jesus when you don't know what's ahead?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean to follow Jesus as a family — even into uncertain situations?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one step of faith your family could take together, even if you're not sure how it will turn out.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus stepping into a wooden boat on calm water, disciples eagerly climbing in after Him.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there arose a great tempest in the sea, insomuch that the ship was covered with the waves: but he was asleep.
verses.little_bible: Suddenly a huge storm hit the lake! The waves were crashing over the boat. But Jesus — was asleep. Completely at peace in the middle of the storm.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A huge storm hit the boat — but Jesus was peacefully asleep!
verses.meaning: The disciples, many of whom were experienced fishermen, were terrified — so this storm was genuinely dangerous. Jesus' sleep was not ignorance but profound peace. He was untroubled by what terrified them.
verses.prayer: Lord, when everything around me feels like a storm, help me trust that You are with me and You are at peace.
verses.discussion_question: How could Jesus sleep in a huge storm? What does that tell you about Him?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples were experienced sailors and they were scared. What is the 'storm' your family faces right now, and how can you invite Jesus into it?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a picture of a boat in a storm with Jesus sleeping inside — then put your name in the boat too.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small wooden boat tossing on enormous dark waves under a lightning-streaked sky, Jesus curled up asleep at the stern, completely undisturbed.

verses.kjv: And his disciples came to him, and awoke him, saying, Lord, save us: we perish.
verses.little_bible: The disciples ran to Jesus and woke Him up, crying, 'Lord, save us — we are going to die!' They were terrified, but they knew exactly where to go.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples woke Jesus crying, 'Lord, save us!'
verses.meaning: Even in their fear, the disciples' instinct was to go to Jesus. The prayer 'Lord, save us' is one of the simplest, most powerful prayers in the Bible — and Jesus answers it.
verses.prayer: Lord, when I am scared, help me run to You and call out 'Save me!' I know You will answer.
verses.discussion_question: When you are really scared, what do you do? What can you learn from the disciples here?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples were right to wake Jesus. God is not bothered when we cry out to Him in panic. When has your family called out to God in a hard moment?
verses.do_it_today: The next time you feel scared today, say out loud: 'Lord, I am here. Please help me.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Disciples grabbing the shoulder of a sleeping Jesus, their faces white with fear, waves towering around the boat.

verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Why are ye fearful, O ye of little faith? And then he arose, and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a great calm.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Why are you afraid? Where is your faith?' Then He stood up and spoke to the wind and the waves — and everything became perfectly still. Instant calm.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus rebuked the storm and everything became completely calm.
verses.meaning: Jesus addresses their fear first (a heart problem) then the storm (an outside problem). 'Little faith' means faith that is real but not yet grown. He is gentle — He does not say 'no faith', just 'little'. Then He calms the storm.
verses.memory_phrase: He rebuked the storm; great calm
verses.prayer: Jesus, grow my faith so I trust You even in scary times. I know You can calm every storm.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus calmed the storm with just His voice. What is something in your life that feels like a storm right now?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus addressed the fear before He addressed the storm. Why might it matter that He cared about their hearts, not just their safety?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you have been worrying about? Give it to Jesus by name today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing at the prow of the boat, arms raised, the wild sea going glassy still all around, disciples watching in stunned silence.

verses.kjv: But the men marvelled, saying, What manner of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him!
verses.little_bible: The disciples were amazed and kept asking each other, 'Who IS this? Even the wind and the waves do what He says!' Jesus has power over everything.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They were amazed: 'Even the wind and waves obey Him!'
verses.meaning: This is the memory verse question of the chapter: Who is this? The disciples are on the edge of understanding. Only God controls the sea — Psalm 107:29. Jesus is doing what only God does.
verses.memory_phrase: Even winds and sea obey Him
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Lord of everything — wind, waves, sickness, and fear. I worship You!
verses.discussion_question: The disciples asked 'What manner of man is this?' How would you answer that question?
verses.family_discussion: This question — Who is Jesus, really? — is the most important question anyone can answer. How does your family answer it together?
verses.do_it_today: Look up Psalm 107:28-30 and see what David said about God and the sea centuries before Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: The disciples in a now-calm boat, staring at Jesus with wide, awed eyes, the still water reflecting the sky around them.

verses.kjv: And when he was come to the other side into the country of the Gergesenes, there met him two possessed with devils, coming out of the tombs, exceeding fierce, so that no man might pass by that way.
verses.little_bible: When they reached the other shore, two men who were controlled by dark spirits came out from the graveyard. They were so frightening that nobody dared walk on that road.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Two very scary demon-possessed men came toward Jesus from the tombs.
verses.meaning: The 'tombs' indicates these men lived among the dead — the darkest, most unclean place in Jewish culture. The dark spirits had driven them to total isolation and terror. But they ran toward Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, nothing is too dark for You. Thank You that You are stronger than anything that tries to hurt or scare us.
verses.discussion_question: If everyone else was afraid of these men, why do you think Jesus went toward them instead of away?
verses.family_discussion: The men were living in the worst kind of darkness. Jesus crossed the lake specifically to reach them. How does knowing Jesus seeks out the lost change how you see people who are struggling?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone who seems lost or in a very dark place.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two wild-eyed figures emerging from stone tomb entrances at dusk, but in the distance Jesus and His disciples approaching calmly.

verses.kjv: And, behold, they cried out, saying, What have we to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God? art thou come hither to torment us before the time?
verses.little_bible: The evil spirits inside them cried out, 'What do You want with us, Jesus, Son of God?! Have You come to punish us before the right time?' Even the demons knew exactly who Jesus was.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The demons cried out, 'Jesus, Son of God — why are you here?'
verses.meaning: The demons acknowledge Jesus' identity with complete accuracy — 'Son of God' — showing that all spiritual forces, even evil ones, know who He is. They also know their eventual judgment is coming.
verses.memory_phrase: Demons know Jesus is the Son of God
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Son of God — even evil knows that. I choose to trust You, not fear darkness.
verses.discussion_question: If even demons recognise who Jesus is, what does that tell you about how important it is to know Him too?
verses.family_discussion: The demons had 'correct theology' — they knew who Jesus was — but they were not saved by that knowledge. What is the difference between knowing about Jesus and truly trusting Him?
verses.do_it_today: Tell God directly: 'I know who You are, Jesus, and I choose You.'
verses.illustration_prompt: The two men recoiling, mouths open in a shriek, arms raised defensively before the calm figure of Jesus.

verses.kjv: And there was a good way off from them an herd of many swine feeding.
verses.little_bible: Not far away, a large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. This detail will matter in a moment.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Nearby, a large herd of pigs was eating on the hillside.
verses.meaning: Pigs were unclean animals in Jewish law, and this area had a mixed population. The pigs' presence is a narrative detail setting up what follows — the demons' request and God's permission.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me notice the small details in life — often they are part of Your bigger story.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Matthew included this detail about the pigs?
verses.family_discussion: In Bible stories, small details are often there for a reason. What do you think God is setting up here?
verses.do_it_today: Look for one 'small detail' in your day and think about whether God might be at work through it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A hillside with a large herd of pigs grazing contentedly, unaware of what is about to happen.

verses.kjv: So the devils besought him, saying, If thou cast us out, suffer us to go away into the herd of swine.
verses.little_bible: The evil spirits begged Jesus, 'If You drive us out, at least let us go into those pigs!' Even the demons had to ask Jesus for permission. Jesus is in charge of everything.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The demons begged Jesus, 'Please let us go into the pigs!'
verses.meaning: The demons begged — they did not command or resist. They were completely under Jesus' authority. Even their request had to go through Him. This underlines His absolute power over all spiritual forces.
verses.prayer: Lord, You are in charge of everything — even things that seem dark and scary. I trust Your authority.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the demons had to ask Jesus for permission?
verses.family_discussion: Nothing in the spiritual world acts outside God's knowledge and permission. How does that truth help you when you are afraid of dark or difficult things?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one thing that feels out of control and then write: 'Jesus has authority over this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: The two wild-looking men gesturing desperately toward the pig herd, with Jesus standing calmly, listening.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Go. And when they were come out, they went into the herd of swine: and, behold, the whole herd of swine ran violently down a steep place into the sea, and perished in the waters.
verses.little_bible: Jesus simply said, 'Go.' The evil spirits left the men and went into the pigs — and the whole herd rushed off the cliff into the lake and drowned. Jesus had won completely.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said 'Go!' — the demons left and the pigs ran into the sea.
verses.meaning: Jesus' one-word command 'Go' settles the encounter instantly. The result shows the destructive nature of evil — even the pigs were destroyed by what had tormented these men. Jesus freed the men at significant cost.
verses.memory_phrase: One word from Jesus is enough
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your one word can change everything. Speak into my life and drive out anything that doesn't belong.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think Jesus' one word 'Go' tells you about His power?
verses.family_discussion: Evil destroyed the pigs when it left the men. What does this show us about what evil does wherever it goes?
verses.do_it_today: Practice memorising the memory verse: 'Even the winds and the sea obey Him.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A herd of pigs plunging off a steep cliff into the lake below, while Jesus stands above watching with quiet authority.

verses.kjv: And they that kept them fled, and went their ways into the city, and told every thing, and what was befallen to the possessed of the devils.
verses.little_bible: The pig farmers ran back to town and told everyone what had happened — both about the pigs and about the two men who had been set free.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The farmers ran to town and told everyone what Jesus had done.
verses.meaning: The news spread rapidly. The herdsmen were witnesses to both the loss of the pigs and the liberation of the men. Their testimony spread the news before Jesus even reached the town.
verses.prayer: Lord, when You do something amazing in my life, give me courage to tell others.
verses.discussion_question: The farmers ran to tell everyone. When was the last time you told someone what Jesus has done for you?
verses.family_discussion: Good news is meant to be shared. How does your family share what Jesus is doing in your lives?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today something Jesus has done for you or your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two farmers running breathlessly toward a walled town, arms waving, people rushing out to meet them and hear the news.

verses.kjv: And all the city came out to meet Jesus: and when they saw him, they besought him that he would depart out of their coasts.
verses.little_bible: The whole town came out to find Jesus — and then they asked Him to leave. They were more worried about their pigs than they were excited about the men who had been set free.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The whole town asked Jesus to leave their land.
verses.meaning: The townspeople's reaction is a sobering one. They chose their economic loss over the miracle of liberation. This is a picture of how people can choose comfort and security over Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, don't let me ever choose comfort or money over You. You are worth so much more.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the people asked Jesus to leave? What were they really saying?
verses.family_discussion: The people were more upset about the pigs than glad about the two men's freedom. What does your family value most — things or people?
verses.do_it_today: Think about one thing you treasure. Could you give it up if Jesus asked you to?
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowd of townspeople standing at the edge of their town, arms raised in a gesture of dismissal toward Jesus, who stands calmly among His disciples ready to leave.
